changes.value:
## What

When multiple windows show the same board, only the `main` window shows the active perspective. Secondary windows show no active selection even though clicking tabs works on `main`.

### Root cause

`PerspectiveProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/perspective-context.tsx`) passes `scopeChain: []` on all `backendDispatch` calls. The backend's `SetActivePerspectiveCmd` (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/ui_commands.rs:192`) extracts the window label via `window_label_from_scope()` — with empty scope it falls back to `\"main\"`. So `active_perspective_id` is only ever written to the `main` window's UIState slot. Secondary windows read their own (empty) slot at line 46-47.

Compare with `ViewsProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/views-context.tsx:38-43`) which correctly passes `scopeChain: [\\`window:${WINDOW_LABEL}\\`]`.

### Fix

In `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/perspective-context.tsx`:

1. **`setActivePerspectiveId` (line 54)** — change `scopeChain: []` to `scopeChain: [\\`window:${WINDOW_LABEL}\\`]`
2. **`refresh` (line 62)** — add `boardPath` from `useActiveBoardPath()` so `perspective.list` targets the correct board when multiple boards are open. Import `useActiveBoardPath` from `@/lib/command-scope`.
3. **Auto-create \"Default\" (line 93)** — same fix: add `scopeChain` with window label

Also fix `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` dispatches that pass `scopeChain: []`:
4. **`handleAdd` (line 68)** — add window label to scopeChain
5. **`commitRename` (lines 94, 98)** — add window label to scopeChain

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Opening two windows on the same board, both show perspective tabs
- [ ] Clicking a tab in window B sets active perspective in window B (not main)
- [ ] Each window independently tracks its own active perspective
- [ ] `perspective.list` includes `boardPath` so it targets the correct board

## Tests
- [ ] Update `perspective-context.test.tsx` assertions to expect `scopeChain: [\"window:main\"]` instead of `scopeChain: []`
- [ ] Update `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx` mock assertions for scopeChain
- [ ] `pnpm test` from `kanban-app/ui/` — all pass
Fix multi-window perspective — scopeChain missing window label, no boardPath

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,32 +1,34 @@
 ## What

-When multiple windows show the same board, only the `main` window shows the active perspective. Secondary windows show no active selection even though clicking tabs works on `main`.
+When multiple windows show the same board, only the `main` window shows the active perspective. Secondary windows show no active selection.

 ### Root cause

-`PerspectiveProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/perspective-context.tsx`) passes `scopeChain: []` on all `backendDispatch` calls. The backend's `SetActivePerspectiveCmd` (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/ui_commands.rs:192`) extracts the window label via `window_label_from_scope()` — with empty scope it falls back to `\"main\"`. So `active_perspective_id` is only ever written to the `main` window's UIState slot. Secondary windows read their own (empty) slot at line 46-47.
+`PerspectiveProvider` uses `backendDispatch` directly with hardcoded `scopeChain: []`. The root `CommandScopeProvider` in `App.tsx:571` already provides `window:${WINDOW_LABEL}` as a moniker, so the scope chain is available via `useContext(CommandScopeContext)` + `scopeChainFromScope()`. But `PerspectiveProvider` doesn't read it — it bypasses the context entirely.

-Compare with `ViewsProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/views-context.tsx:38-43`) which correctly passes `scopeChain: [\\`window:${WINDOW_LABEL}\\`]`.
+The backend's `SetActivePerspectiveCmd` falls back to `\"main\"` when no `window:` moniker is in the scope chain. So all windows write to the `main` slot.

 ### Fix

 In `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/perspective-context.tsx`:

-1. **`setActivePerspectiveId` (line 54)** — change `scopeChain: []` to `scopeChain: [\\`window:${WINDOW_LABEL}\\`]`
-2. **`refresh` (line 62)** — add `boardPath` from `useActiveBoardPath()` so `perspective.list` targets the correct board when multiple boards are open. Import `useActiveBoardPath` from `@/lib/command-scope`.
-3. **Auto-create \"Default\" (line 93)** — same fix: add `scopeChain` with window label
+1. Import `useContext` for `CommandScopeContext` and `scopeChainFromScope` from `@/lib/command-scope`
+2. Read the scope chain from context: `const scope = useContext(CommandScopeContext); const chain = scopeChainFromScope(scope);`
+3. Use `chain` in all `backendDispatch` calls instead of `scopeChain: []`
+4. Also read `boardPath` from `ActiveBoardPathContext` and pass it so `perspective.list` targets the correct board
+
+Same fix in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx`:
+5. The `handleAdd` and `commitRename` calls also hardcode `scopeChain: []` — read from context instead

-Also fix `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` dispatches that pass `scopeChain: []`:
-4. **`handleAdd` (line 68)** — add window label to scopeChain
-5. **`commitRename` (lines 94, 98)** — add window label to scopeChain
+This follows the same pattern as `useExecuteCommand` at `command-scope.tsx:285-301`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Opening two windows on the same board, both show perspective tabs
+- [ ] Opening two windows on the same board, both show perspective tabs with active selection
 - [ ] Clicking a tab in window B sets active perspective in window B (not main)
 - [ ] Each window independently tracks its own active perspective
 - [ ] `perspective.list` includes `boardPath` so it targets the correct board

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Update `perspective-context.test.tsx` assertions to expect `scopeChain: [\"window:main\"]` instead of `scopeChain: []`
-- [ ] Update `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx` mock assertions for scopeChain
+- [ ] Update `perspective-context.test.tsx` assertions to expect `scopeChain: [\"window:main\"]` (from mocked scope context)
+- [ ] Update `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx` if needed
 - [ ] `pnpm test` from `kanban-app/ui/` — all pass
\ No newline at end of file
changes.reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,34 +1,32 @@
 ## What

-When multiple windows show the same board, only the `main` window shows the active perspective. Secondary windows show no active selection.
+When multiple windows show the same board, only the `main` window shows the active perspective. Secondary windows show no active selection even though clicking tabs works on `main`.

 ### Root cause

-`PerspectiveProvider` uses `backendDispatch` directly with hardcoded `scopeChain: []`. The root `CommandScopeProvider` in `App.tsx:571` already provides `window:${WINDOW_LABEL}` as a moniker, so the scope chain is available via `useContext(CommandScopeContext)` + `scopeChainFromScope()`. But `PerspectiveProvider` doesn't read it — it bypasses the context entirely.
+`PerspectiveProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/perspective-context.tsx`) passes `scopeChain: []` on all `backendDispatch` calls. The backend's `SetActivePerspectiveCmd` (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/ui_commands.rs:192`) extracts the window label via `window_label_from_scope()` — with empty scope it falls back to `\"main\"`. So `active_perspective_id` is only ever written to the `main` window's UIState slot. Secondary windows read their own (empty) slot at line 46-47.

-The backend's `SetActivePerspectiveCmd` falls back to `\"main\"` when no `window:` moniker is in the scope chain. So all windows write to the `main` slot.
+Compare with `ViewsProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/views-context.tsx:38-43`) which correctly passes `scopeChain: [\\`window:${WINDOW_LABEL}\\`]`.

 ### Fix

 In `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/perspective-context.tsx`:

-1. Import `useContext` for `CommandScopeContext` and `scopeChainFromScope` from `@/lib/command-scope`
-2. Read the scope chain from context: `const scope = useContext(CommandScopeContext); const chain = scopeChainFromScope(scope);`
-3. Use `chain` in all `backendDispatch` calls instead of `scopeChain: []`
-4. Also read `boardPath` from `ActiveBoardPathContext` and pass it so `perspective.list` targets the correct board
+1. **`setActivePerspectiveId` (line 54)** — change `scopeChain: []` to `scopeChain: [\\`window:${WINDOW_LABEL}\\`]`
+2. **`refresh` (line 62)** — add `boardPath` from `useActiveBoardPath()` so `perspective.list` targets the correct board when multiple boards are open. Import `useActiveBoardPath` from `@/lib/command-scope`.
+3. **Auto-create \"Default\" (line 93)** — same fix: add `scopeChain` with window label

-Same fix in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx`:
-5. The `handleAdd` and `commitRename` calls also hardcode `scopeChain: []` — read from context instead
-
-This follows the same pattern as `useExecuteCommand` at `command-scope.tsx:285-301`.
+Also fix `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` dispatches that pass `scopeChain: []`:
+4. **`handleAdd` (line 68)** — add window label to scopeChain
+5. **`commitRename` (lines 94, 98)** — add window label to scopeChain

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Opening two windows on the same board, both show perspective tabs with active selection
+- [ ] Opening two windows on the same board, both show perspective tabs
 - [ ] Clicking a tab in window B sets active perspective in window B (not main)
 - [ ] Each window independently tracks its own active perspective
 - [ ] `perspective.list` includes `boardPath` so it targets the correct board

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Update `perspective-context.test.tsx` assertions to expect `scopeChain: [\"window:main\"]` (from mocked scope context)
-- [ ] Update `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx` if needed
+- [ ] Update `perspective-context.test.tsx` assertions to expect `scopeChain: [\"window:main\"]` instead of `scopeChain: []`
+- [ ] Update `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx` mock assertions for scopeChain
 - [ ] `pnpm test` from `kanban-app/ui/` — all pass
